page_content:
BIOE340 Modeling Physiological Systems and Lab (4 Credits) Topics covered will include cell and general physiology, membrane physiology, blood cells and clotting, circulation, metabolism, respiration, and the nervous system. A lab component will also be included. Prerequisite: Minimum grade of C- in BSCI330 and BIOE331. Restriction: Permission of ENGR-Fischell Department of Bioengineering department. Credit Only Granted for: BIOE340 or (BSCI440 and BSCI441).

BIOE371 Linear Systems and Ordinary Differential Equations for Bioengineering Applications (3 Credits) This class utilizes fundamentals in linear systems, including eigenvalues and eigenvectors, as well as linear differential equations, to study various problems in bioengineering and biological systems, with a particular emphasis on feedback, stability, controllability, and control design. Prerequisite: BIOE241; and must have completed or be concurrently enrolled in MATH246. Restriction: Permission on ENGR-Fischell Department of Bioengineering department.
